name: Loaded "Baked" Potato Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 cups shredded sharp cheddar cheese, plus additional for garnish
1 cup sour cream, plus additional for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook bacon in a Dutch oven over medium heat until crisp, about 8 minutes. Remove and set aside.
2. Use a vegetable peeler to remove wide strips of potato peel; reserve the peels. Cut peeled potatoes into 3/4-inch pieces.
3. Add reserved potato skins to the bacon fat in the pot and cook until crisp, about 8 minutes. Transfer to a plate with the bacon.
4. Add onion to the remaining fat and cook over medium heat until golden, about 6 minutes. Stir in garlic and flour, cooking until fragrant, about 1 minute.
5. Gradually whisk in broth and cream, stirring in thyme and potatoes. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce to medium-low. Cover and cook until potatoes are tender, about 7 minutes.
6. Discard the thyme and transfer 2 cups of cooked potatoes to a bowl. Puree the remaining soup in batches using a blender until smooth. Return to the pot and warm over medium-high heat.
7. Off the heat, stir in cheese until melted, then whisk in sour cream. Return reserved potatoes to the pot and season with pepper.
8. Serve the soup in bowls, garnishing with bacon, fried potato skins, scallions, cheese, and sour cream.
Makes 8 servings.
